question: what is ayushman bharat?
final_answer: Ayushman Bharat, also known as Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY), is a flagship scheme of the Government of India launched to achieve Universal Health Coverage. It aims to provide financial protection to the poor and vulnerable families at the time of illness and to ensure that no citizen is denied access to quality healthcare due to lack of funds. The scheme provides a health cover of up to ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. It is an entitlement-based scheme, meaning it is for those who are identified as beneficiaries based on certain criteria. The scheme is implemented by the National Health Authority (NHA) and aims to cover over 10 crore families across India.
eligibility: Eligibility for Ayushman Bharat is primarily determined by the deprivation and occupational criteria of the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011 data. Families included in the 'Deprivation' categories and 'Occupational' categories of the SECC 2011 are considered eligible. You can check your eligibility by visiting the official Ayushman Bharat website (pmjay.gov.in) or by contacting the nearest empanelled hospital or a Common Service Centre (CSC). You can also call the toll-free helpline number 14555 or 1800-111-565.
benefits: Health cover of up to ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
Includes expenses for medicines, diagnostics, and consumables.
Covers medical and non-medical expenses like travel, food, and attendant expenses.
No cash payment at the hospital; services are cashless.
Covers a wide range of medical and surgical procedures.
how_to_apply: Check your eligibility using your Aadhaar number or mobile number on the official PMJAY website (pmjay.gov.in) or by calling the helpline.
If eligible, visit an empanelled public or private hospital with your proof of identity and eligibility (like an Aadhaar card or ration card).
The hospital will assist you in the verification process and enrollment. You will receive an e-card upon successful enrollment.
documents_required: Proof of Identity (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card, Driving License, Passport)
Proof of Eligibility (e.g., Ration Card, SECC 2011 ID card, or any other document confirming your inclusion in the SECC 2011 database)
practical_tips: Always verify your eligibility through official channels to avoid misinformation.
Carry your Aadhaar card or other valid ID proof when visiting a hospital for treatment under Ayushman Bharat.
Ask the hospital staff about the Ayushman Bharat scheme and how to avail the benefits.
Keep the PMJAY helpline number handy for any queries or assistance.
